sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Diana Scott, is a 100-year-old woman who presents with a new diagnosis of a heart murmur.
She reports that her doctor told her she has a worrisome heart murmur, but she is unsure of the severity or type.
She denies any symptoms of chest pain or shortness of breath, but reports severe pain in her legs, which started about a month ago.
She also reports feeling tired and taking medication for her legs.
She denies any fever, chills, or other symptoms.
She reports a 70-80% increase in symptoms over the past six months, with no symptoms six months ago.
She reports feeling lightheaded and dizzy, but takes medication for it.
She denies any passing out or irregular heartbeats.
She reports swelling in her ankles, which has been present for about six months, and has lost weight over the past year.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of multiple surgeries, and her son had a heart attack.
Her brother had a stroke due to sugar diabetes, and her eldest sister had a heart problem.
Social history: The patient never smoked and has no known allergies to medications.
Review of systems: The patient reports severe leg pain, fatigue, lightheadedness, and dizziness.
Current medications:
Amlodipine 10 mg daily for hypertension
Lisinopril 5 mg daily for hypertension
Simvastatin 20 mg daily for hyperlipidemia
Levothyroxine for hyperthyroidism
Meclizine for lightheadedness and dizziness
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient's physical exam is as follows: Constitutional elderly, otherwise no acute distress.
Cardiovascular: Normal S1 and S2 with a normal rate and regular rhythm.
A 4/6 systolic murmur at the right upper sternal border with mild radiation to the carotids.
Neurologic: Gait is normal for age.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a cardiac murmur with a 4/6 systolic murmur at the upper right sternal border with mild radiation to the carotids, which is most likely representative of aortic stenosis.
Problem list: Cardiac murmur; suspected aortic stenosis; pvcs; hypolipidemia; lightheadedness/dizziness.
Plan:
I prescribed amlodipine 10 mg daily for hypertension and lisinopril 5 mg daily for hypertension to continue as currently prescribed.
I will defer to the patient's primary care physician regarding her simvastatin for hyperlipidemia, but consider stopping it due to bilateral leg pain.
I will send the results of the echocardiogram and my recommendations to the patient's primary care physician.
I will schedule the patient for an echocardiogram to assess her cardiac structure and function, as well as the valve view of her heart.
I will discuss the results and next steps with the patient in three to four weeks.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up in three to four weeks to discuss the results of the echocardiogram and next steps.
